comments.body: FORCE_CLOSE: close gate(s) bypassed by user approval â€” no completion/signature attestation was signed. Reason: "All 3 children (S1 2ee4, S2 40b8, S3 8451) closed with signed plan-review + completion-verifier attestations; work landed on main via Gerrit feature branch (changes 260/261/262 + merge-back 263, both bot gates MAX). Verifier's two objections resolved on facts: (1) no_tool fail-open proven empirically â€” with pyright absent from PATH, refute() returns abstain/no_tool/T2 and version()=None; (2) 'children not closed' is a false-negative (all three show status=closed + completion-verifier attestation). Epic is a rollup; substantive signed proof lives on the children.". Session: 392408f19.

compiled_state.description:
## Context

Fills the **T2 semantic-resolution tier** deferred out of the code-grounding v1 floor (epic
`8f6c-6008-33f3-4f82`, closed). v1 shipped the deterministic, polyglot, fail-open **T0
(manifest/registry existence) + T1 (ctags/tree-sitter syntactic)** floor plus a **T2
abstain-by-default seam**: `TIER_T2` exists in the closed tier vocabulary but is never emitted, and
member/dotted references abstain inline in `resolve.refute_absence` (tagged "member binding is T2").
This epic wires a real, self-contained, opt-in T2 backend behind that seam **without changing the
three-valued evidence contract (`refuted` / `match` / `abstain`) or its consumers**.

Constraints any T2 backend must respect (why T2 was deferred):
- **Not deterministic by nature.** Semantic resolution is a function of machine state (installed deps,
  build state, runtime versions), not just the artifact. Every failure mode
  (not-installed / not-built / wrong-version / hung) is indistinguishable from a genuinely fake symbol.
- **Confirm-only verdict asymmetry.** A T2 `refuted` (an asserted absence disproved â€” the reference
  resolves) is trustworthy; an asserted absence is suspect unless the environment is known-good. So
  T2 emits a trustworthy `refuted` and otherwise **`abstain`s** (the canonical outcome for
  "unknown") â€” it never sits on a deterministic blocking path.
- **Cannot be borrowed from the ambient environment.** No reliance on an externally-configured MCP
  server (e.g. Serena) or a host toolchain; the backend is self-contained + opt-in.

> **Terminology (locked to the code vocabulary).** The three-valued outcome is
> `OUTCOME_REFUTED="refuted"` / `OUTCOME_MATCH="match"` / `OUTCOME_ABSTAIN="abstain"` (`evidence.py`).
> "present" is the informal gloss for `refuted` (absence disproved); "unknown" is the informal gloss
> for `abstain`. This epic writes `refuted` / `abstain` everywhere; the glosses appear only in prose.

## Decided v1 design

**Selected v1 backend: one-shot compiler/checker-CLI diagnostics** (candidate 2), starting with
`pyright --outputjson` for Python. Rationale over the alternatives: no long-lived server (simplest to
fail-open + batch), no ambient dependency (the checker is an opt-in extra / PATH probe), and it yields
a trustworthy `refuted` when the checker runs cleanly. The **bundled LSP-client library** (candidate
1) and **containerized/hermetic** (candidate 3) tiers are **deferred** â€” the LSP path multiplies the
per-language server/runtime/config surface; the containerized path's gating cost is an egress-locked
single-tenant microVM sandbox (arbitrary-code-execution on untrusted client repos). Both remain future
backends behind the same seam this epic establishes.

## Design specifics (resolving the plan-review indeterminates)

**The seam is a dispatch function, not a plugin registry (Rule-of-Three).** With one concrete backend
shipping, a general `T2Backend` Protocol + registry is speculative abstraction. S2 instead adds one
module `src/rebar/grounding/semantic.py` exposing:

```python
def refute_semantic(reference, *, repo_root, config, timeout=None) -> dict | None
def available_backends() -> list[dict]   # [{name, available, version}] for grounding_info, fail-open
T2_BACKENDS: tuple[str, ...] = ("pyright",)   # the closed set of selectable backend names
```

`refute_semantic` returns a **normalized T2 evidence record** (`refuted@T2` or `abstain@T2`) or
**`None`** meaning "T2 declined to run â€” fall back to the caller's T1 record" (default-off path). A
future backend adds a name to `T2_BACKENDS` and a branch in `refute_semantic` â€” no premature Protocol.

**Escalation call-site (single owner): `oracle.refute_absence`.** After the T1/deps lanes return,
if the record is an `abstain` on a `member`/dotted reference or a not-found bare `symbol`/`import`,
`oracle.refute_absence` calls `semantic.refute_semantic(...)`; a returned `refuted@T2` replaces the
T1 abstain, otherwise the T1 record is returned unchanged. `resolve.refute_absence` (the standalone
T1 lane) is **not** modified â€” keeping T2 out of the T1 lane preserves T1's determinism.

**Config schema** (`.rebar/grounding.toml`, `[grounding]`, read by `resolve.load_config`; default-off):
- `t2_enabled` : bool, default `false` â€” the master opt-in.
- `t2_backend` : string in `T2_BACKENDS`, default `"pyright"` â€” an unknown value fails open to
  disabled (abstain), never a raise.
- `t2_timeout_seconds` : number, default `30` â€” the bounded per-invocation subprocess timeout.

**`grounding_info` change is explicit code, not free.** `oracle._backend_availability()` is a
hardcoded list today; S2 appends `*semantic.available_backends()` to it. The `grounding_info` schema's
`backends[]` name field is an open string, so a new backend name validates without a schema change
(S2 adds a conformance assertion proving this).

**pyright invocation model (the trustworthiness crux).** pyright is invoked **over the project root**
(not single-file) via the fail-open `harness.run_tool` with `t2_timeout_seconds`, so cross-module
imports resolve. The confirm-only decision for a reference in file F:
- **`refuted@T2`** iff: pyright ran and produced parseable JSON, **AND** F has **zero**
  import-resolution diagnostics (`reportMissingImports` / `reportMissingModuleSource` â€” the
  "environment built" precondition), **AND** there is **no** diagnostic at the reference's
  span/name (`reportAttributeAccessIssue` / `reportUndefinedVariable` / unresolved-import).
- **`abstain@T2`** (closed reason) in every other case: tool absent (`no_tool`), non-Python
  reference (`unsupported_lang`), no locatable file (`ambiguous`), unparseable output
  (`parse_error`), timeout (`timeout`), crash (`other`), F has unresolved imports so the env is
  not-built (`other`, detail records it), or a diagnostic sits at the reference (`other` â€” a
  suspected-absent is never asserted). One pyright run per review is cached by project root and
  reused across references (no per-reference re-invocation).

If real pyright output does not cleanly partition these diagnostic codes, the backend **abstains**
(fail-safe): S3's tests pin the mapping against captured real `--outputjson` fixtures, and any
unmapped/ambiguous code routes to `abstain`, never `refuted`.

## Related: IaC grounding lane â€” split out

The Terraform `validate`/Registry/`plan` tiering is a distinct domain (not a code-build environment)
â€” it becomes its **own sibling epic** (created by S1, linked `relates_to`), not a child here.

## Decomposition (3 stacked children, bottom â†’ top; landed as one Gerrit feature branch)

- **S1 â€” ADR + IaC split (design/doc):** an ADR under `docs/adr/` recording the backend selection,
  the confirm-only mapping, the minimal-seam (no-registry) decision, and the deferral of
  LSP-lib/containerized; plus creation of the sibling IaC epic.
- **S2 â€” T2 seam (default-off):** `semantic.py` (`refute_semantic` / `available_backends` /
  `T2_BACKENDS`), the three config keys, escalation in `oracle.refute_absence`, and
  `_backend_availability()` reporting â€” all default-off so behavior is byte-identical when
  `t2_enabled=false`, proven by a regression test.
- **S3 â€” pyright diagnostics backend:** the Python backend `refute_semantic` dispatches to, the
  optional `grounding-t2` extra, docs (`docs/grounding.md` T2 section + config keys), and a
  per-backend fail-open matrix pinned against real pyright `--outputjson` fixtures.

## Success Criteria

- One self-contained, opt-in T2 backend (pyright) implements the v1 seam and emits `TIER_T2` evidence
  records that validate against `grounding.schema.json`, with zero edits to the evidence contract or
  its consumers (`5fd2` DET floor, `9da1` reviewers).
- Every T2 outcome is `refuted` (trustworthy) or `abstain` (a closed structured reason); no code path
  emits an asserted absence.
- With `t2_enabled=false` (the default) the full grounding test suite is byte-identical to pre-epic
  behavior (regression test proves it); T2 never appears on a deterministic blocking path.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] S1 lands an ADR under `docs/adr/` that names the v1 backend (pyright compiler-CLI diagnostics),
      records the confirm-only mapping and the minimal-seam decision, and creates + links a sibling
      IaC epic; the LSP-lib and containerized tiers are recorded as deferred with their tradeoffs.
- [ ] S2 lands `semantic.refute_semantic`/`available_backends`/`T2_BACKENDS`, the three
      `.rebar/grounding.toml` keys, the `oracle.refute_absence` escalation, and the
      `_backend_availability()` reporting â€” with `t2_enabled=false` producing byte-identical evidence
      to pre-epic behavior, proven by a default-off regression test in `tests/unit/grounding/`.
- [ ] S3 lands the pyright backend that emits `refuted@T2` when pyright runs, the file has zero
      import-resolution diagnostics, and there is no diagnostic at the reference; and emits
      `abstain@T2` with a closed reason for tool-absent / non-Python / no-file / parse-error /
      timeout / crash / env-not-built / diagnostic-at-reference.
- [ ] No code path emits an asserted absence: verified by a test asserting `refute_semantic` returns
      only `refuted` or `abstain` outcomes across the fail-open matrix.
- [ ] Self-contained + opt-in: the backend is gated behind the `grounding-t2` optional extra plus
      `t2_enabled`, and returns `abstain(no_tool)` (never a raise) when pyright is absent â€” proven by
      a test that runs with pyright off `PATH`.
- [ ] Fail-open matrix passes for the pyright backend (unsupported lang / no tool / no locatable file
      / crash / timeout / diagnostic-present / env-not-built â†’ `abstain`), pinned against captured
      real `pyright --outputjson` fixtures.
- [ ] `grounding_info` (CLI `grounding-info`, MCP `grounding_info`, library `rebar.grounding_info()`)
      lists the pyright T2 backend with its detected availability/version, and the output still
      validates against `grounding_info.schema.json`.
- [ ] Proving command green: `pytest tests/unit/grounding/ && make lint` passes; all three children
      (S1â€“S3) closed and landed on `main` via the Gerrit feature branch.

## Planned file impact

- `docs/adr/00NN-code-grounding-t2-semantic-resolution.md` (S1, new ADR)
- `src/rebar/grounding/semantic.py` (S2, new T2 dispatch seam)
- `src/rebar/grounding/resolve.py` (S2, `GroundingConfig` + `load_config` T2 keys)
- `src/rebar/grounding/oracle.py` (S2, escalation + `_backend_availability`)
- `src/rebar/grounding/pyright_backend.py` (S3, the pyright backend)
- `pyproject.toml` (S3, `grounding-t2` optional extra)
- `docs/grounding.md` (S3, T2 section + config keys)
- `tests/unit/grounding/` (S2 regression + S3 fail-open matrix)
compiled_state.title: Code-grounding T2: self-contained semantic-resolution backends behind the abstain-by-default seam (LSP-lib / compiler-CLI / containerized)
